cro.window_note: DECISIVE: dossier frames a 'Q4-2026 readout' but the actual binary is later and slipped — ECLIPSE 1 reaches primary endpoint Q4-2026 with TOPLINE DATA Q1-2027, and ECLIPSE 2/3 topline also Q1-2027 (Vir guidance). To clear it with the mandated +1 monthly margin a call needs Mar/Apr-2027+ expiries on a ~180-contract/day chain — long-dated, illiquid, IV-bleed. staging:true / dated_milestone:null confirms there is no hard date to size an expiry around. A correct thesis on an instrument too slow/thin for its option = the loss-with-correct-thesis case the mandate forbids paying for.

cro.window_note: DECISIVE: staging:true, dated_milestone:null, and the dossier itself states 'no near-term event to size around' — REZPEG Phase 3 atopic-dermatitis durability data is a Q4-2026+ / 2027 story with no hard date. picks.entry_rationale: Only FDA_clinical_readout survivor that is hard-dated (Q3-2026 LUCIDITY avexitide topline, 109d) AND non-staging, so the mandated defined-risk option for a dated binary is actually constructible; ev_pct 0.4512 at win 0.62 with edge surviving the +12% run-up (payoff still >half-dossier).
picks.invalidation: Negative or ambiguous LUCIDITY topline (PBH avexitide miss) drives equity toward the $4 floor; defined premium is the max loss. Also killed if a clean defined-risk call cannot be built at acceptable OI/spread and the only path is naked equity exposure to the floor.
picks.review_trigger: LUCIDITY avexitide PBH Phase 3 topline, Q3 2026 (conservative milestone 2026-09-30); confirm option leg OI/spread at open before lifting.
picks.resolution_driver_note: binary_prob - sized off ev_pct at the 2% premium-at-risk cap, not the 1.66x payoff.

Cannot build the mandated defined-risk leg, so the seat goes to the one dated binary (AMLX) where defined-risk is constructible.

director.memo: Headroom was the whole story. The locked held book already maxes FDA_approval_decision (AQST+VRDN) and Forced_divest_flow (FIG+BLCO) at 2/2 each, which kills 5 survivors outright (ZYME, BBIO, CERS, MGNI, CLVT). Of the remaining 8, ALL share the single open driver FDA_clinical_readout (held CELC leaves exactly 1 slot), so at most ONE new seat is addable — the per-driver cap, not edge or cluster weight (FDA/biotech had 28.5 pts free), is binding. Within that pool I took AMLX over the higher-edge EYPT because the mandate forces binaries into a DEFINED-RISK structure: EYPT's (and most of the pool's) options are untradeable, leaving only naked equity exposure to the floor — which fails the gate. AMLX is the one hard-dated (Q3-2026, 109d), non-staging binary where a defined_risk_option clearing the milestone by +1 monthly (Oct/Nov-2026) is actually constructible, sized at the 2% premium-at-risk cap off ev_pct 0.4512. Result: 1 new seat (10/12 total), FDA/biotech cluster 11.5→13.5 of 40, FDA_clinical_readout now 2/2. Shape stays a small, defined-risk biotech-binary tail-add on top of a held book that already carries the deal-completion and idiosyncratic weight — no cluster concentration, no cap breach, no forced seat.
